item.title: Appeals court keeps in place Pentagon's escort policy for reporters
item.description: The appeals court in Washington, D.C., divided 2-1 in ruling in favor of the Trump administration in the legal battle over the Pentagon's escort policy for journalists.

item.title: When is hiring a debt relief company worth the cost?
item.description: Pursuing debt relief can be a smart move but it isn't always worth paying for. Here's when it could pay off.

item.title: Tennis icon Novak Djokovic talks aging in the sport and what he still wants to accomplish
item.description: Tennis legend Novak Djokovic talks with "CBS Mornings" co-host Gayle King about continuing to play at an elite level at the age of 39, Serena Williams' return to tennis and what he still hopes to accomplish in his career.

item.title: India activist urged to end hunger strike as he risks organ failure
item.description: Well-known activist Sonam Wangchuk hasn't eaten in nearly 3 weeks, endangering his health as he demands changes to India's exam system.

item.title: Maine Democratic Senate candidates criticize ICE during debate
item.description: Maine Democrats will choose a new Senate nominee after Graham Platner's exit from the race. This comes after an ICE officer in Maine fatally shot a man, renewing heated discussions over the role of the agency. CBS News' Lilia Luciano reports.

item.title: "Worst case scenario" for shippers as U.S. and Iran step up attacks
item.description: As the U.S. and Iran escalate attacks 140 days into the war, an industry analyst says "nobody is willing to move" through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: "Master yachtsman" fugitive captured on boat after 21 years on the run
item.description: Ronald L. Fischer, who was featured on "America's Most Wanted," fled Rhode Island during his criminal trial for first-degree sexual assault in 2005.

item.title: Dangerous flooding in Texas leads to more than 230 water rescues
item.description: More than 2 feet of rain slammed the Texas Hill Country in the past four days, causing dangerous floods. Jason Allen reports.

item.title: How much interest can a $10,000 CD account earn if opened this July?
item.description: A $10,000 CD can generate serious interest — but only with the right term. Here's what to know about the math.

item.title: Fact-checking Trump's speech on election security
item.description: President Trump delivered a speech on election security Thursday night at the White House. Here are the facts behind some of his claims.

item.title: 26 million pre-menopausal women in U.S. are impacted by uterine fibroids
item.description: Uterine fibroids are noncancerous tumors that develop in the muscle of the uterus. In the U.S., the medical condition affects more than 26 million pre-menopausal women. Dr. Tara Shirazian explains how she's helping to guide other doctors through educational videos. She also discusses how patients can educate themselves and treatment options.

item.title: Breaking down Trump's primetime speech on election security
item.description: During his speech, President Trump alleged Chinese interference in the 2020 election, a claim contradicted by U.S. intelligence agencies. CBS News' Weija Jiang reports, and election law contributor David Becker weighs in on Mr. Trump's claims. Plus, CBS News' Anthony Salvanto takes a look at the latest polling to see what Americans think.

item.title: Fact-checking Trump's repeated election fraud claims in primetime address
item.description: President Trump criticized the U.S. election system and claimed China interfered in the 2020 election without providing any evidence during his primetime address. CBS News' Aaron Navarro, Jarred Hill and Anna Coren have more details.

item.title: Torrential rain brings deadly flooding to Texas Hill Country
item.description: Torrential downpours dumped more than two feet of rain in Texas Hill Country in just four days, causing deadly flooding. CBS News' Jason Allen reports.

item.title: U.S. strikes Iran for sixth consecutive night
item.description: The U.S. military carried out strikes on Iran for the sixth night in a row. Iranian media said U.S. strikes targeted infrastructure, including bridges and a train station. CBS News' Courtney Kealy reports.

item.title: A new pill could help millions of people lower their cholesterol
item.description: The FDA has approved a pill form of a powerful class of medication that targets bad LDL cholesterol. Chief medical correspondent Dr. Jon LaPook explains.

item.title: 100 million Americans across 14 states impacted by heavy smoke from Canadian wildfires
item.description: Smoke from wildfires in Canada is creating dangerous air quality conditions for approximately 100 million Americans in more than a dozen states. Rob Marciano has the latest.

item.title: Wildfires, dangerous air quality "will become more and more prevalent," climate scientist warns
item.description: Millions of people are enduring dangerous air quality as Canadian wildfire smoke shrouds the skies. Jonathan Overpeck, dean of the School for Environment and Sustainability at the University of Michigan, explains how climate change is worsening wildfires.

item.title: White House teleprompter operator allegedly made over $100K betting on Trump's speeches
item.description: The prediction market Kalshi alleges President Trump's longtime teleprompter operator engaged in trading, betting on the words the president would use in speeches. Jo Ling Kent reports.

item.title: Who is Andy Burnham, the man becoming Britain's next prime minister?
item.description: The man replacing U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer is Andy Burnham, known as the "King of the North."

item.title: Lettuce supplier for Taco Bell investigated as possible source for cyclosporiasis outbreak
item.description: The FDA has identified a single supplier of iceberg lettuce from Mexico as the possible source of the cyclosporiasis outbreak that has sickened thousands of people, a source says. Taco Bell uses that supplier in five states where people have gotten sick. Ash-har Quraishi reports.

item.title: Fact-checking Trump's claims about election security during his primetime speech
item.description: During his primetime speech on Thursday, President Trump outlined alleged Chinese efforts to collect voter data and repeated disproven claims about voting by noncitizens. Weijia Jiang and CBS News election law contributor David Becker have more on the president's claims.

item.title: Andy Burnham declared Labour Party leader, will become U.K. prime minister
item.description: Andy Burnham was officially declared leader of Britain's governing Labour Party, clearing his final hurdle to taking office as prime minister next week.

item.title: Tree-killing Asian beetle detected in EU for first time
item.description: The emerald ash borer, which has devastated ash forests in North America, has been detected in the European Union for the first time.

item.title: Trump revisits disputed claims about elections but offers no new proof of fraud
item.description: In a primetime address, President Trump alleged the U.S. election system falls "catastrophically short," revisiting a topic that has drawn his attention for years — and making claims that election experts have heavily disputed.

item.title: Cyclosporiasis outbreak possibly traced to lettuce from Mexico used by Taco Bell
item.description: A lettuce supplier to fast-food giant Taco Bell is being investigated as a possible source for a nationwide cyclosporiasis outbreak that has sickened thousands of people.

item.title: Teleprompter operator made over $100K betting on Trump speeches
item.description: After detecting suspicious activity on the teleprompter operator's account, Kalshi investigated and then referred the case to federal regulators.

item.title: Nearly 736,000 Pillsbury rolls recalled over possible glass contamination
item.description: The recall includes cases of Pillsbury "Hard Roll Dough" and "Kaiser Roll Dough" bread rolls, which are marketed to businesses.

item.title: Trump Media to sell faster access to president's Truth Social posts
item.description: "Markets already move on Truth Social posts," the company's chief executive said in the Truth API announcement.

item.title: "Jurassic Park" star Sam Neill's cause of death revealed
item.description: The actor's agent said he was providing more information following news reports "which contain inaccuracies and outright falsehoods."
